confirms.delete_user: Delete user {username}? This cannot be undone.
confirms.safe_defaults: Auto-tune memory settings for the currently loaded model and restart the engine?
confirms.force_restart: Kill the inference engine and start it fresh? In-flight requests will be lost.
confirms.unsaved_changes: You have unsaved changes. Discard them?

toolbar.files_soon: File attachments are coming in v1.3 (RAG per agent — upload PDFs / TXT / MD). For now, paste text directly into the message.
toolbar.mcp_soon: MCP integration is on the roadmap for v1.3. It will let agents call external tools and APIs via the standard Model Context Protocol.

providers.title: Cloud AI providers
providers.intro: Configure API keys to use external models (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) from the same chat interface. The active provider is picked from the dropdown next to the chat mode at the top.
providers.label: Provider

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
mcp.intro: MCP is an open standard for connecting AI assistants to tools, file systems, databases, and external APIs in a uniform way. Agents that support MCP can read NAS files, query databases, search the web, run scripts, and call any service that exposes an MCP server.
mcp.coming_intro: MCP integration is planned for the next release. When shipped, you'll be able to:
mcp.feat_servers: Register MCP servers by URL or local command (stdio).
mcp.feat_tools: Pick per-agent which tools (resources, prompts, functions) the model can call.
mcp.feat_filesystem: Bundled servers: NAS filesystem (allow-listed paths), HTTP fetch (allow-listed hosts), calculator, current time, simple web search.
mcp.feat_observability: Per-call audit log so you can see which tools the agent invoked and with what arguments.
mcp.unavailable: MCP runtime unavailable on this install.
mcp.manual_note: ⚠ Current state: tools run manually from this panel (pick a tool → Run). The assistant does not yet auto-call tools during chat — that is the next milestone. The runtime + 8 servers below are fully functional when invoked here.
mcp.roadmap: In-chat tool-calling (model invokes tools automatically), plus Tier-B cloud integrations: web search (Brave/Tavily), GitHub, Home Assistant, Synology FileStation, Surveillance Station — those will need per-user API keys.

about.dsm_hint: Updates today: use this button, or download the SPK from the Releases page and install via Package Center → Manual Install. (One-click DSM Package Source updates are planned but need a dynamic backend — GitHub Pages can't answer DSM's requests yet.)

connection.server_url_hint: Leave empty when opening the chat from the DSM icon. Set to http://NAS-IP:8181 when accessing from another device.
connection.poll_interval_hint: How often to ping the server. Higher = less network noise.
connection.streaming_hint: When enabled, the assistant's reply appears word-by-word. Disabled by default because llama.cpp b1620 returns HTTP 500 on some streaming combinations; non-streaming is reliable.

setup.title: Create the first admin account
setup.sub: This account will own the package configuration. You can add more users later.
setup.username_hint: At least 3 characters. Lowercase letters & digits recommended.

login.title: Welcome back
login.sub: Sign in to chat with your local AI.

chat.welcome_title: How can I help you today?
chat.welcome_sub: Local model on your NAS GPU. Conversations stay in your browser.
chat.connection_error_hint: Is the model running? Check Package Center → Filamind AI Chat → Run.

chat: Write a short poem about the sea.
What are 5 healthy lunch ideas under 500 calories?
Tell me an interesting fact about the universe.
Help me draft a thank-you note for a colleague.
code: Write a Python function to fetch JSON from an API with retries.
Refactor this code to be more readable: [paste code]
How do I set up a basic Node.js Express server?
writer: Help me brainstorm a blog post about remote work.
Edit this paragraph for clarity: [paste paragraph]
Suggest 5 titles for an article about climate change.
Write a 200-word LinkedIn post about leadership.
research: Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms with an analogy.
Compare the pros and cons of React vs Vue.
Walk me through how mRNA vaccines work.
What are the main causes of inflation?
translate: Translate to Arabic: 'Hello, how can I help you today?'
Translate to French: 'The package will arrive tomorrow.'
Translate to Japanese: 'Thank you very much for your time.'
Translate this paragraph to Spanish: [paste text]

settings.title: Settings
settings.system_prompt: System prompt (overrides agent preset if set)
settings.system_prompt_hint: Persistent instructions sent to the model at the start of every conversation.
settings.max_tokens_hint: 512 is a good default. Very high values can make small models ramble or hold the engine slot.

resources.apply_hint: These settings are applied by restarting the inference engine. Save below to apply.
resources.gpu_layers_hint: 999 = all layers on GPU. Lower for CPU offload.
resources.port_hint: Changing the port requires restarting the package from DSM.
resources.mlock_hint: Prevents the OS from paging the model out. Recommended on systems with plenty of RAM.
resources.no_mmap_hint: Loads the entire model into RAM up-front. Faster, uses more RAM.
resources.extra_args_hint: Advanced: extra command-line flags appended to llama-server.
resources.safe_defaults_title: Auto-tune ctx-size & GPU layers for the current model size
resources.vram_hint: For the GTX 1650 (4 GB VRAM): models ≤2 GB can use ctx 8192; ~4 GB models need ctx 2048; >4 GB models offload some layers to CPU. Use "safe defaults" if a model fails to load with "Server offline".

models.none_found: No .gguf files found in any search directory.
models.click_to_switch: Click any model to switch. Place new .gguf files in /volume1/FilamindAI/models/ (via File Station) and they'll appear here automatically.
models.download_section: Download a model from Hugging Face
models.download_hint: Curated GGUF models that work with this build (Llama, Mistral & Phi-2 family). The file lands in /volume1/FilamindAI/models/.
models.control_api_unreachable: Could not reach control API: {error}. Open the chat URL from DSM (icon) for full functionality.

system.force_restart_confirm: Kill the inference engine and start it fresh? In-flight requests will be lost.

agents.title: Agents (chat personas)
agents.intro: Each agent has a system prompt + sampling defaults + optional preferred local model. Switch between agents from the sidebar. Edit built-in agents or create your own.
agents.system_prompt_placeholder: You are a helpful assistant who…

chat.name: Friendly Assistant
chat.description: Warm, conversational AI for general questions.
chat.system_prompt: You are a helpful, friendly, and conversational AI assistant. Reply in a natural, warm tone. Be concise unless the question requires depth.

coder.name: Code Master
coder.description: Expert programmer. Clean, idiomatic code with concise explanations.
coder.system_prompt: You are an expert software engineer. Write clean, idiomatic code in the requested language. Add brief comments only where non-obvious. Prefer modern best practices. When debugging, explain the root cause concisely. Always use Markdown code fences with language tags.

writer.name: Writer & Editor
writer.description: Drafting, editing, and polishing prose with vivid language.
writer.system_prompt: You are an experienced writer and editor. Help with drafting, structuring, and polishing prose. Use vivid language when fitting, but keep clarity first. When asked for feedback, point to specific lines and suggest concrete improvements.

researcher.name: Researcher
researcher.description: Careful, step-by-step analysis. Cites reasoning openly.
researcher.system_prompt: You are a careful research assistant. Think step by step. When uncertain, say so. Cite reasoning openly. For complex questions, break them down before answering. Avoid speculation that is not clearly labeled.

translator.name: Polyglot Translator
translator.description: Accurate translation preserving tone and intent.
translator.system_prompt: You are a professional translator. Translate the user's text accurately. Preserve tone, formality, and intent. If the target language is not specified, ask. Provide ONLY the translation unless context is requested.

tutor.name: Patient Tutor
tutor.description: Explains concepts simply with examples and analogies.
tutor.system_prompt: You are a patient tutor. Explain concepts in plain language with concrete examples and analogies. Adapt to the learner's level. After each explanation, ask if anything needs clarification. Never assume prior knowledge.

storyteller.name: Storyteller
storyteller.description: Vivid narrative fiction with strong sensory detail.
storyteller.system_prompt: You are a creative storyteller. Write vivid, sensory-rich fiction. Use dialogue and pacing to keep readers engaged. Build characters with specific quirks. Show, don't tell.

email.name: Email Helper
email.description: Professional, friendly email drafts for any situation.
email.system_prompt: You write professional emails. Match the requested tone (formal, friendly, direct). Keep it concise. Start with the point, support with one or two sentences, end with a clear next step. No filler or jargon.

brainstormer.name: Brainstormer
brainstormer.system_prompt: You are a brainstorming partner. When asked for ideas, give a numbered list of at least 7 diverse options. Range from safe and obvious to wild and unconventional. Each idea is one short sentence. No preamble, no caveats — just ideas.

summarizer.name: Summarizer
summarizer.description: Compresses long text into clear bullets.
summarizer.system_prompt: You compress text. Read what the user provides, then output a tight summary as bullet points. Capture the core argument, key facts, and conclusion. Do not add information that is not in the source. Stay under 200 words unless asked otherwise.

math.name: Math & Reasoning
math.description: Step-by-step problem solving with careful reasoning.
math.system_prompt: You are a careful mathematician. Solve problems step by step. Show every step explicitly. Verify your answer at the end. If a question is ambiguous, restate your assumptions. Use plain notation (no LaTeX commands unless the user asked).

marketer.name: Copywriter
marketer.description: Punchy marketing copy and product descriptions.
marketer.system_prompt: You write marketing copy. Hook, benefit, proof, call to action — in that order. Short sentences. Strong verbs. No hype words like 'revolutionary' or 'cutting-edge'. Match the user's product voice.

admin.title: Admin
admin.confirm_delete: Delete user {username}? This cannot be undone.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.api_keys_desc: Generate keys for programmatic access. Use them as Bearer tokens against /v1/ endpoints.
profile.key_created: Key created — copy it now, you won't see it again.
profile.new_key_hint: Choose a label so you can revoke individual keys later (e.g. "Cursor", "n8n", "Home Assistant").
profile.copy_prefix_hint: Copy the visible prefix (the full key was shown only once on creation).
profile.connection_desc: Plug these values into any OpenAI-compatible client (OpenWebUI, LibreChat, Continue, Cursor, LangChain, OpenAI SDK …) to call this NAS as a drop-in OpenAI endpoint.
profile.base_url_hint: Most clients call this the "API base URL" or "endpoint URL". It already includes the /v1 prefix.
profile.auth_header_hint: Replace YOUR_API_KEY with a key from the section below. Format: sk-… (45+ chars).
profile.default_model_hint: Use "auto" to let the daemon pick the active local model. Cloud providers expect their own model IDs (e.g. gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4, gemini-2.5-flash).
